title: hebe × simmonsii
intro: Hebe × simmonsii, also known as the Simmons' Hebe, is a hybrid plant species in the genus Hebe, commonly found in New Zealand. It is a shrub-like plant with attractive foliage and flowers, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine. The plant contains flavonoids and other compounds that have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, including the potential to alleviate digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Hebe × simmonsii is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusion, and more recently, it has been used in modern herbal preparations, such as capsules and extracts.
